medications.indication_rationale: Adrenaline is the ONLY first-line treatment for anaphylaxis — reverses airway oedema, bronchospasm and hypotension. In telehealth the AI Doctor must instruct immediate use of any available in-date adrenaline autoinjector (device is pre-dosed) into the outer thigh, and repeat after 5 minutes if no improvement, while 000 is called.
medications.dose_route_frequency: adrenaline autoinjector 300 microgram IM outer mid-thigh (device pre-dosed; 150 microgram junior device if that is what is available for a smaller child) — repeat after 5 minutes if no response

medications.indication_rationale: An oral antihistamine must NOT be used as the primary treatment for anaphylaxis — it does nothing for airway oedema or shock and giving it in place of adrenaline is a dangerous error of commission that delays definitive care. (Antihistamines have only a minor adjunct role for residual urticaria after adrenaline, in hospital.)

Salbutamol treats bronchospasm but NOT the upper-airway oedema or the distributive shock, and must not be mistaken for the treatment — reaching for a reliever inhaler instead of adrenaline is the classic asthma-mimic trap. It is at most an adjunct for persistent wheeze after adrenaline.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Check adrenaline autoinjector expiry dates monthly and replace promptly; always carry two in-date devices.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: The failure point today was an expired, misfired device — a working autoinjector on hand is life-saving at the moment of exposure.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Strict peanut avoidance and allergen-checking of baked goods eaten away from home.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents re-exposure; the trigger was an unlabelled baked good at a friend's house.

patient_education_points.point: Adrenaline is the only fast treatment that opens the airway and lifts the blood pressure — antihistamines and inhalers are not substitutes.
The reaction can return hours later (biphasic), which is why hospital observation is mandatory even after improvement.
Stay seated or lying down — standing during anaphylaxis can cause a sudden dangerous drop in blood pressure.

follow_up_plan.interval: immediately (ambulance transport now) then allergy clinic within 2–4 weeks
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: airway, breathing, circulation, biphasic reaction over a minimum 4-hour observation; then specialist allergy work-up and action plan
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: any recurrence of airway symptoms, breathing difficulty, faintness or collapse — second adrenaline dose and continue emergency care

must_include_items: Recognise anaphylaxis from the multi-system constellation after a known allergen
Instruct IMMEDIATE use of any available in-date adrenaline autoinjector into the outer thigh
Call 000 for emergency ambulance without delay
Instruct the patient to stay seated/lying — do not stand or walk
Advise repeat adrenaline after 5 minutes if no improvement while awaiting ambulance
acceptable_alternatives: If no autoinjector is available at all, the emphasis shifts entirely to immediate 000 and positioning — the AI cannot supply adrenaline.
errors_of_omission: Failing to instruct adrenaline autoinjector use
errors_of_commission: Recommending an oral antihistamine or a salbutamol inhaler as the primary treatment instead of adrenaline
Advising the patient to drive themselves to hospital
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES adrenaline-autoinjector instruction AND 000 escalation. Omitting either is a critical failure regardless of other content.
